phrases.intent_en: Ask what makes this quarter special
phrases.text_local: Qu'est-ce qui rend le Quartier Mazarin unique par rapport au reste d'Aix ?
phrases.pronunciation: kes-KUH kee rahn luh kar-TYAY mah-zah-REHN oo-NEEK par rah-POH oh REST DEX
phrases.cultural_note: Unlike the medieval organic layout of Vieil Aix, the Quartier Mazarin was designed from scratch in 1646 with a precise grid, wide streets, and uniform aristocratic architecture. It is quieter, more residential, and less touristy than the old town.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the Four Dolphins fountain
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve la fontaine des Quatre Dauphins ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lah fon-TEN day KAT-ruh doh-FEHN
phrases.cultural_note: The Fontaine des Quatre Dauphins (1667) at the heart of the quarter is one of the most photographed spots in Aix. The four stone dolphins spray water towards a central pine cone. The surrounding square is lined with beautiful hôtels particuliers.

phrases.intent_en: Ask to find the Musée Granet from the Quartier Mazarin
phrases.text_local: Comment rejoindre le Musée Granet depuis ce quartier ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN ruh-ZHWEHN-druh luh moo-ZAY grah-NEH duh-PWEE suh kar-TYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Musée Granet is on the eastern edge of the Quartier Mazarin on Place Saint-Jean-de-Malte, next to the church of the same name — itself worth a look for its Gothic architecture.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a quiet café in this neighbourhood
phrases.text_local: Connaissez-vous un café tranquille dans le Quartier Mazarin ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-nes-SAY voo uhn kah-FAY trahn-KEEL dahn luh kar-TYAY mah-zah-REHN
phrases.cultural_note: The streets of the Quartier Mazarin are far less crowded than the Cours Mirabeau. Several independent cafés and bistros operate here, offering a local experience away from the tourist trail — prices are also usually lower.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the boundary with the Cours Mirabeau
phrases.text_local: Le Cours Mirabeau sépare-t-il le Quartier Mazarin du Vieil Aix ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh KOOR mee-rah-BOH say-PAR-teel luh kar-TYAY mah-zah-REHN doo VYEHY EKS
phrases.cultural_note: Yes — the Cours Mirabeau forms the precise boundary between the medieval Vieil Aix to the north and the classical Quartier Mazarin to the south. Crossing from one side to the other is like stepping between two different centuries of urban planning.
